finding: Vehicle-type breadth attenuates the Local MaaS high-unlock-fee penalty
evidence: For Local MaaS, unlock_fee_ge_1_00:local_maas_only:no_of_vehicle_types_z is positive in 6/6 models; all market FE = 0.074 (p < 0.001), all unit FE = 0.014 (p < 0.001), no-weak market FE = 0.052 (p < 0.001), and scooter market FE = 0.029 (p = 0.0067). The EUR 1.20 interaction is positive in 5/6 models, with all market FE = 0.075 (p < 0.001) and all unit FE = 0.014 (p < 0.001).
interpretation: Product assortment appears to buffer Local MaaS high-fee penalties: when more vehicle types are available, the negative impact of salient unlock fees is weaker.
caveat: This is a moderation result, not a main effect; it should be framed as product breadth reducing pricing-friction harm.

finding: Vehicle-type breadth does not consistently buffer the multi-platform high-fee penalty
evidence: For multi-platform, no_of_vehicle_types_z is positive at EUR 1.00 in unit FE (all unit FE = 0.065, p < 0.001), but the EUR 1.20 scooter-only unit-FE interaction turns negative (-0.021, p < 0.001).
interpretation: Product breadth is not a stable multi-platform buffer; the clean assortment story is Local MaaS-specific.
caveat: Avoid generalizing the product-breadth moderation claim to all platform architectures.

finding: Group ride tends to amplify Local MaaS high-fee penalties within units
evidence: group_ride triple interactions are negative for Local MaaS in unit-FE models: EUR 1.00 all unit FE = -0.039 (p < 0.001), no-weak unit FE = -0.035 (p < 0.001), scooter unit FE = -0.026 (p < 0.001); EUR 1.20 all unit FE = -0.011 (p = 0.003), no-weak unit FE = -0.007 (p = 0.048), scooter unit FE = -0.021 (p < 0.001).
interpretation: Group ride does not soften the pricing-friction problem; in Local MaaS contexts it may further mark a usage mode or provider position where high upfront fees are especially harmful.
caveat: Support is narrow because high-fee Local MaaS observations often have group_ride = 1, especially at EUR 1.20.

finding: Complementary services cannot be tested as a high-fee buffer with current support
evidence: For Local MaaS and multi-platform high-fee rows, complementary_services has zero nonzero observations in the support table. Coefficients around zero with tiny p-values are collinearity artifacts.
interpretation: Complementary services should not be used to explain the high-unlock-fee platform penalty with current data.
caveat: Requires richer provider/service coding before reuse.
